research_methodology: Direct URL verification via HTTP + browser navigation. Search engines blocked at VPS IP (176.123.22.210). 35+ URLs tested, 12 verified live with real calculator content. Tool roundup articles proved hardest to find — most old roundup posts deleted or moved. Two found via Motley Fool and Calculator.net resource pages.

prospects.problem: Nonprofit credit counseling site with basic embedded calculators (Debt Management, Loan, Budgeting). Their embedded tools are functional but dated — static HTML calculators with limited interactivity. A modern interactive widget would better serve their clients in debt management programs.
prospects.evidence: Page verified live (HTTP 200, 574KB). Three calculator sections found: 'Debt Management Calculator', 'Financial and Loan Calculators', 'Budgeting Calculator'. These are standard embedded tools — no interactive debt snowball, no amortization visualization, no mobile-optimized experience.
prospects.evidence_quality: HIGH — page live, content verified, calculator types confirmed
prospects.why_asset_fits: GreenPath provides DMP (Debt Management Plan) services. Their clients need to visualize debt payoff strategies. QFINHUB's debt snowball widget shows exact payoff order, interest savings, and timeline — directly relevant to their core service.
prospects.personalization_angle: Your debt management clients would benefit from a visual debt snowball tool that shows exactly how extra payments accelerate their DMP. Complements your existing counseling services.
prospects.send_ready_message: I noticed your financial wellness page offers calculators for debt management and budgeting. I have a free interactive debt snowball widget that visualizes payoff strategies for each client — would be a great addition for your DMP participants. No signup needed — want me to send the embed code?

summary_stats.research_notes: Search engines (Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo) blocked from VPS IP 176.123.22.210. 45+ URLs tested directly via HTTP. Tool roundup articles proved hardest to find — most old roundup posts on finance blogs have been deleted (404) or moved. Motley Fool's calculator hub and MoneyFit's page function as de facto roundups. No verified broken calculator links found — dinkytown.net is still alive (HTTP 200). Flash-based calculators not discovered. Credit union and nonprofit calculator pages represent the 'outdated replacement' opportunity.
